title: pyrus × kewensis
intro: Pyrus × kewensis, also known as the Kew crabapple, is a sterile hybrid plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is a cross between the Pyrus calleryana and Pyrus communis species. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains tannins that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of Pyrus × kewensis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce swelling, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation of the plant typically involves drying and processing the bark to create a tincture or infusion that can be used to treat various health conditions.
